decision_summary: The V6 slice is ready because reference and starter traces both reach release_readiness and the post-release boundary remains explicit.

launch_roles.responsibility: Approve the V6.0 promotion for lifecycle traceability through release_readiness.
Confirm the starter workspace remains the reusable adoption surface for the promoted V6 slice.
Confirm the trace command and workspace snapshots agree on delivery, launch, outcome, and full-lifecycle coverage.

claim_readiness.claim: The V6 bundle proves lifecycle traceability through release_readiness in both adoption surfaces.
Launch preparation and outcome review remain explicit but out of scope for the promoted V6 claim.

checks.name: Reference workspace lifecycle trace reaches release_readiness
checks.notes: The reference workspace exposes one item-first lifecycle trace from discovery through delivery planning and release_readiness.

checks.name: Starter-workspace adoption parity through release_readiness
checks.notes: The starter workspace mirrors the selected lifecycle-traceability path through release_readiness.

checks.name: Trace focus-area coverage
checks.notes: All advertised trace focus areas now have concrete workspace snapshots behind the CLI surface.

checks.name: Scoped boundary after release_readiness
checks.notes: Launch preparation and outcome review remain explicit but not_started, which keeps the V6 claim bounded to release_readiness.
